summary:
Significant high-risk regulatory changes impact food seasoning/snack compliance across multiple regions. **GCC** introduced new requirements for general labeling, Halal food, mandatory expiration dates, and microbiological limits, all requiring specific GSO standard verification. **Japan** added fundamental hygiene rules, prohibited toxic substances, mandated notification for harm from designated ingredients, and set strict limits for pathogen contamination and rotten/deteriorated food. **Singapore** now prohibits non-permitted additives, sets new contaminant limits (including specific veterinary drug residues), and requires full ingredient/additive declaration. **Taiwan** implemented mandatory allergen labeling for 10 specific allergens and sulphite (>=10 mg/kg). The **United States** has new prohibitions on delisted color additives and other substances, updated its major food allergen list (now including sesame), set an action level for total aflatoxins (20 ppb), and introduced new labeling rules for 'gluten-free' claims, allergen preventive controls, cochineal extract/carmine, major food allergen declaration, FD&C Yellow No. 5, and sulfiting agents (>=10 ppm). 

Moderate-risk additions include new rules in **China** (food additive use, GB standards), **Codex** (permitted additives, contaminant limits), **EU** (food additives, microbiological limits), **GCC/GSO** (pre-packaged food labeling, shelf life), **India** (food additives, general labeling), **Indonesia** (various additive, allergen, contaminant, categorization, microbiological, and salt/sodium limits), **Japan** (imported food/additive controls, permitted additives, production methods, packaging, pesticide/veterinary drug residues), **Singapore** (Class II preservatives, general additives, pesticide residues, various contaminants, microbiological standards), **Taiwan** (pesticide residues, nutrition claims, precautionary allergen labeling, GABA use), and **Thailand** (permitted additives, contaminants, general/nutrition labeling). Several moderate-risk rules were also removed due to source fetch/extraction issues in China, Codex, EU, GCC, Indonesia, Japan, Malaysia, Middle East, Philippines, Singapore, Taiwan, and Thailand, requiring verification.

changes.notes: Specific labelling requirements for prepackaged food products, including mandatory information, must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 9:2022.
General requirements for Halal food, including sourcing, processing, and labelling, must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 2055-1:2015.
Mandatory expiration date requirements for food products must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 150-1:2013.
Specific microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 1016:2015.

changes.parameter: General hygiene for food and additives
changes.newLimit: Must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
changes.notes: This is a fundamental requirement to prevent contamination.

changes.newLimit: Not allowed to contain or bear toxic or harmful substances, or be suspected to, unless specified by MHLW as harmless.

changes.parameter: Notification of harm from designated ingredients
changes.newLimit: Businesses handling food containing MHLW-designated ingredients must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc., without delay if they obtain information that the food causes or may cause harm to human health.
changes.notes: This is a mandatory notification requirement for potential safety hazards.

changes.parameter: General hygiene for food and additives
changes.newLimit: Must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
changes.notes: This is a fundamental requirement to prevent microbiological contamination.

changes.newLimit: Not allowed to be contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, if likely to cause harm to human health.
Not allowed to be rotten or deteriorated, unless unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.

changes.notes: Food products containing an additive that is not permitted must not be imported or sold.
Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.
Import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing residues of specific oestrogen compounds is prohibited.
For prepacked foods, all ingredients and additives used must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

changes.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
changes.newLimit: Required if present as ingredient or food additive
changes.notes: Mandatory allergen labeling for these items when present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.

changes.newLimit: 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food
changes.notes: Mandatory allergen labeling for sulphite when the residue sulphur dioxide concentration is 10 mg/kg or more in prepackaged foods.
Delisted color additives are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) and the Substances Added to Food inventory.
Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) and the Substances Added to Food inventory.
The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. All FDA requirements apply to sesame as of January 1, 2023.
This is an action level for total aflatoxins in general foods. Specific food categories like Brazil nuts, peanuts, pistachio nuts also have this action level.
FDA has a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling (August 2, 2013), including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (August 12, 2020).

The CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. Facilities must have written procedures.
Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

The food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement. Specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food at a concentration of 10 ppm or more total sulfites.
This standard specifies the varieties, scope of use, and maximum dosages of food additives. The full standard document must be consulted for detailed limits.

changes.parameter: Compliance with GB Food Safety Standards
changes.notes: The National Health Commission is responsible for developing and overseeing food safety standards (GB standards) in China. Specific standards must be consulted for detailed requirements.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the online database and its Preamble for specific provisions.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
changes.notes: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration legally permitted. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
Food additives approved for use in the EU and their conditions of use must comply with the Union list of food additives, which is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
changes.notes: Food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria for foods are laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
This regulation specifies the mandatory information that must be present on the labels of pre-packaged food products.

changes.parameter: Mandatory shelf life periods for food products
changes.notes: This regulation specifies the mandatory shelf life periods for various food products, which must be adhered to and declared on labels.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.newLimit: Verification required as per FSSAI regulations.
changes.notes: Specific additive limits for various food categories, including seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks, are detailed in the FSSAI Compendium of Food Additives Regulations. Verification of the specific additive and food category is required.

changes.newLimit: Verification required as per FSSAI regulations.
changes.notes: General labelling and display requirements for food products, including seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks, are detailed in the FSSAI Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations. Verification of specific labelling elements is required.

This circular letter outlines the provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive. Specific limits would be detailed within the full regulation.
BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which include provisions for food additive limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.
BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which likely include provisions for allergen declaration. Specific requirements must be verified within these regulations.
BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which include provisions for contaminant limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.
This regulation defines food categories, which are fundamental for applying specific rules and for correct product labelling.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which include provisions for microbiological limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.
BPOM has 281 regulations concerning processed foods, which may include provisions for salt/sodium limits. Specific limits must be verified within these regulations.

changes.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring specific process controls or certification
changes.newLimit: Must be produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities, or have certificates from the exporting country. Verification required.
changes.notes: This applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.

changes.newLimit: Only additives specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm to human health are permitted for sale, production, import, processing, use, storage, or display. Verification required.
changes.notes: Excludes natural flavoring agents and substances generally served for human consumption and used as additives.

changes.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food or additives; ingredients of food or additives
changes.newLimit: Must conform to criteria and standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
changes.notes: These standards and criteria can cover various aspects including additive limits, microbiological safety, and contaminant levels.

changes.newLimit: Must be clean, sanitary, and not contain or bear toxic or harmful substances, or have a harmful effect on food/additives. Must conform to MHLW-established standards/criteria. Verification required.
changes.notes: This applies to apparatus, containers and packaging used in business or sold.

changes.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring specific process controls or certification
changes.newLimit: Must be produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities, or have certificates from the exporting country. Verification required.
changes.notes: This applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.

changes.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food or additives; ingredients of food or additives
changes.newLimit: Must conform to criteria and standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
changes.notes: These standards and criteria can cover various aspects including additive limits, microbiological safety, and contaminant levels.

changes.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and animal pharmaceuticals
changes.newLimit: Must not remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm to human health, or established standards. Verification required.
changes.notes: This applies unless specific standards for residual limits are established under Article 13, paragraph (1).
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring specific process controls or certification
changes.newLimit: Must be produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities, or have certificates from the exporting country. Verification required.
changes.notes: This applies to food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.

changes.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food or additives; ingredients of food or additives
changes.newLimit: Must conform to criteria and standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
changes.notes: These standards and criteria can cover various aspects including additive limits, microbiological safety, and contaminant levels.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
changes.notes: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used in food, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used to determine permitted levels.
Food additives must undergo safety assessment by SFA before being used in food sold in Singapore.
Pesticide residues must comply with Singapore's regulatory limits or Codex MRLs if no local limits are specified. Verification is required using the relevant SFA documents or Codex MRLs.

changes.parameter: Various contaminants (Microbiological, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins, Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents)
changes.notes: Verification of specific limits for various contaminants is required by referring to the relevant SFA documents.

changes.parameter: Veterinary drug residues (other than prohibited oestrogens)
changes.notes: Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues have been established and must be complied with. Verification is required by downloading the MRLs from the SFA website.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
Verification of microbiological standards for ready-to-eat food is required by referring to the relevant SFA documents.
Specific limits for pesticide residues in foods must be verified in the referenced standard.
Specific limits for pesticide residues in animal products must be verified in the referenced standard. Relevant if animal products are used as ingredients.
Specific requirements for nutrition claims on prepackaged food products must be verified.
A precautionary allergen labeling statement is suggested if there is a risk of cross-contamination during production.
Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.parameter: Permitted food additives and their limits
changes.notes: Specific lists and limits for food additives must be verified against official notifications and regulations.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.parameter: Contaminants, toxins, and veterinary drug residues
changes.notes: Specific limits for contaminants, toxins, and veterinary drug residues must be verified against official food standards.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.parameter: Toxic Substances and Veterinary Drug Residues
changes.notes: Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.parameter: General food labeling and nutrition labeling
changes.notes: Requirements for food labeling and nutrition labeling must be verified against official regulations.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.oldLimit: Prohibited from production, import, or sale. Verification required for specific prohibited foods.
changes.notes: Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
Specific limits for pathogenic microorganisms must be verified against official food standards.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
Food and color additives are listed in FDA regulations and inventories, requiring verification for specific uses and limits.

changes.parameter: Food additives, color additives, GRAS substances, prior-sanctioned substances
changes.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes various regulated ingredients, requiring verification against specific CFR parts.
This is a guidance level for 3-MCPD.

changes.parameter: Poisonous or deleterious substances (e.g., toxic elements, mycotoxins, persistent pesticides, radionuclides)
changes.notes: The tool provides a consolidated list of contaminant levels including tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.

Advisory statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated and precautions taken. They must be truthful and not misleading.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.newLimit: Follow Code of Practice for Prevention and Reduction
changes.notes: A specific Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.
Guidelines for voluntary expiration dates for food products must be verified by consulting the full text of GSO 150-2:2013.
Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

changes.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined/purified sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products
changes.newLimit: Recommended if present as ingredient or food additive
changes.notes: Food allergen labeling is recommended for these items when present as an ingredient or food additive.
